codeReview.summary: The runtime-parent-child-channels feature has been implemented comprehensively. The implementation adds channel-based communication between parent and child tasks via the ChannelManager, with 22 tests covering all expected behaviors. The code follows existing patterns, properly handles concurrent access, and includes convenience methods for ergonomic parent-child messaging.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker added comprehensive documentation at .factory/library/parent-child-channels.md covering architecture, API methods, message format, scoping guarantees, and downstream feature dependencies. This is a good pattern that other features should follow.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Created .factory/library/parent-child-channels.md (110 lines) documenting: architecture overview, key methods (PostChildResult, PostChildError, PostChildProgress, WaitForChildResult), message format, channel scoping (VAL-CHOIR-015), event emission, auto-channel creation, and downstream features (etext-research-button, concurrent-workers, worker-failure-isolation).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker correctly followed the pattern of adding structured logging with log.Printf() for channel operations, matching the existing codebase conventions. This is visible in the SpawnTask integration and notifyParent method.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: internal/runtime/runtime.go:248-251 - 'runtime: ensure channels for spawned task %s: %v' and internal/runtime/runtime.go:595 - 'runtime: notify parent %s of child %s completion: %v'. Both follow the [component]: message format with relevant identifiers.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker followed the go-worker skill procedure correctly: wrote tests first (TDD approach with 22 tests), implemented the feature, ran validators, and documented thoroughly. The handoff shows skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true with no deviations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows: 'skillFeedback': {'followedProcedure': true, 'deviations': []}. Test file was created first (internal/runtime/parent_child_channel_test.go with 1034 lines), then implementation added to channels.go and runtime.go. All 22 tests passed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The implementation correctly reuses and extends the existing ChannelManager infrastructure. The worker understood that ChannelManager already existed from prior work (runtime-tool-loop-channels) and built parent-child helpers on top of it rather than creating duplicate infrastructure.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The new methods (PostChildResult, PostChildError, PostChildProgress, WaitForChildResult) all leverage existing ChannelManager.Channel(), AgentChannel.Post(), AgentChannel.ReadSince(), and AgentChannel.Wait() primitives. The implementation properly extends rather than duplicates.

summary: The runtime-parent-child-channels feature is complete and well-implemented. All 5 expected behaviors from the feature description are satisfied: (1) Child task can send message to parent via channel - implemented via ChannelPost and convenience methods, (2) Parent can subscribe to messages from specific child - implemented via WaitForChildResult with filtering, (3) Results delivered through channel when child completes - implemented via notifyParent calling PostChildResult/PostChildError in executeTask completion paths, (4) Channel scoped to parent-child relationship - verified by tests showing channels are keyed by work/task ID with proper isolation, (5) Message format {from, to, type, payload} - implemented as ChannelMessage with From, Role (type), Content (payload), and channel key (to). The 22 tests provide excellent coverage including concurrent access, event emission, spawn integration, error notification, and filtered waiting. The implementation properly fulfills VAL-CHOIR-006 (channel-based result collection) and VAL-CHOIR-015 (cross-task event channel safety).
